Trina Roderick for plaintiff and moving party Janet Posada

HEARING HELD and tentative ruling adopted: DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO QUASH DEPOSITION SUBPOENA. The Court has read and considered the memorandum of points and authorities in opposition to the motion. Counsel for plaintiff shall not serve any motion or opposition to any motion by facsimile unless expressly agreed to by defendant. The Defendant's motion to quash deposition subpoena is granted. Plaintiff failed to serve a valid notice of deposition. The Court notes that the subpoena is overbroad. Requests #3 - 9 seek information that is either privileged or irrelevant to the subject matter of this action as indicated by the previous ruling relating to plaintiff's request for production of documents. Requests #1, 2, 10, 11, 12 are overbroad as they seek either privileged information or information that is not specific to 1567 Dolores Street.

The Court finds that plaintiff used a discovery method in a manner that does not comply with its specified procedures and failed to make a reasonable and good faith effort to informally resolve the matters in dispute in light of the prior ruling. Monetary sanctions in the amount of $835 are to be paid within 21 days of notice of this order. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN
